mission.title: harbor: Fix buildkit cache export to harbor.
mission.description:

This fixes goharbor/harbor#14774

Thank you for contributing to Harbor!

# Comprehensive Summary of your change

# Issue being fixed
Fixes #14774

resolution.summary:
Fixes the issue where the caching didn't work for building dev and production container. According to the log, it's due to 404 when GHA tries to push docker image manifest to harbor: https://github.com/PelicanPlatform/pelican/actions/runs/7975774289/job/21774758514

The fix was found here: https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/18105#issuecomment-1811951274

Also adding debug to buildx for more verbose log.
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request

Is the holdup the failing code coverage checks? If so I'd be willing to try and update the tests so they cover the new condition added.
FWIW I have had success pushing the cache to Harbor by setting the new `image-manifest=true` key that was added in https://github.com/moby/buildkit/pull/3724
This PR is being marked stale due to a period of inactivty. If this PR is still relevant, please comment or remove the stale label. Otherwise, this PR will close in 30 days.
not stale
@kiorky Why is it needed to hack the media types in the code? (If buildkit were to change e.g. "application/vnd.buildkit.cacheconfig.v0" to "application/vnd.buildkit.cacheconfig.v1", it would stop working.)
This PR is being marked stale due to a period of inactivty. If this PR is still relevant, please comment or remove the stale label. Otherwise, this PR will close in 30 days.
Not stale.
We're currently running into this exact same issue also.
Would also like to see this merged so that we can use buildkit cache using harbor.
I'm still waiting on this to merge as well and would be happy to help if I can make that happen.

In case this helps anyone else, here are the steps I'm taking outside of harbor to get around this for now:

1. Create a buildx builder using the latest `moby/buildkit:master`
- This is required because, last time I tried, the default would not let me use the `image-manifest` arg required for the fix in the next step
2. When specify the `cache-to` argument set `image-manifest=true`
- Example `docker-bake.hcl`:

You can add this arg similarly to a command line call just as well. Hope this helps.
using Buildkit version 0.12 or higher will indeed work with the steps as mentioned by @bnjmn.
I'm not quite understanding what's stopping you from using harbor caching already?
